data.title: Why Is Nexera Technologies Stock (NEXR) Up Today?
data.description:
Nexera Technologies (NEXR) stock rocketed higher after the homeland security and e-commerce advanced technology company announced an update from subsidiary KeepZone AI. KeepZone AI has received an official letter of authorization from a protective infrastructure solutions provider. This will allow it to introduce and represent the provider's composite structural survivability systems for fuel storage tanks and critical energy infrastructure with certain clients in the Gulf region.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nexera Technologies noted that the provider's patented system allows for extra protection of fuel storage tanks and critical energy infrastructure via a special coating. This coating adds dynamic blast mitigation, fragment protection, spall control, secondary containment, long-term ultraviolet stability, and corrosion resistance. It has an expected lifespan of 25 years.

Alon Dayan, Chief Executive Officer of KeepZone AI, said, "We believe this authorization represents a significant step forward in KeepZone's expansion into critical infrastructure protection in the strategically important Gulf region. By promoting advanced protective technology, we are positioning KeepZone to help energy operators enhance national resilience and safeguard vital assets against modern threats."

Nexera Technologies Stock Movement Today

Nexera Technologies stock was up 91% in premarket trading on Tuesday, following an 8.7% rally yesterday. However, the stock has fallen 82.62% year-to-date and 99.01% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of NEXR stock, as more than 16.6 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 169,000 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Intel (INTC) Just Had a Blowout Quarter. Here Is Why I'm Not Chasing the Stock
data.description:
Intel (INTC) just had a blowout quarter, but I am not chasing the stock after the recent run. The Q1 results announced on April 23 clearly showed that demand for artificial intelligence (AI) is picking up, especially in data center central processing units (CPUs), and confirmed that Intel is back in the AI conversation. However, following the massive double-digit rally after Q1, much of that improvement now appears to be priced in. As a result, the path forward becomes more dependent on execution.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

About a year ago, when the stock was trading around $20, it would have seemed hard to imagine INTC reaching the high double digits it is trading at today. In this article, I share some insights into the leading semiconductor manufacturer's recent momentum and explain why I remain more skeptical of the thesis, especially on valuation grounds.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq Futures Slip on Report of OpenAI Missing Targets; Oil Prices Rise
data.description:
Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index trended lower on Tuesday morning after the Wall Street Journal reported that OpenAI missed its revenue and new user targets, with CFO Sarah Friar expressing concerns that the AI startup may not be able to pay computing contracts if its top line doesn't grow fast enough. Futures on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.12% at 8:35 a.m. EDT on April 28, while futures tied to the Nasdaq 100 and the S&P 500 were down 1.42% and 0.76%, respectively.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

In a favorable development, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed on Monday that Donald Trump and his national security team are reviewing Iran's proposal. The plan offers to reopen the Strait of Hormuz if current conflicts end and U.S. sanctions are lifted.

Meanwhile, oil prices continued to rise amid persistent tensions in the Middle East. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was up by 3.43% to $111.88 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) gained 4.11% to $100.34.

In Monday's regular trading session, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq set new intraday and closing records to kick off a busy week, with the indices up 0.1% and 0.2%, respectively, while the Dow slipped 0.1%.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of Oracle (ORCL), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Broadcom (AVGO), Nvidia (NVDA), and several AI stocks were down in Tuesday's pre-market trading in reaction to the news of weakness in OpenAI's revenue and new user growth.

General Motors (GM) stock rose 1% after the automaker beat Q1 earnings expectations and raised full-year guidance. Coca-Cola (KO) stock rose 3% after the beverage giant surpassed Q1 expectations. Spotify (SPOT) stock plunged 10% after the music streaming platform issued weaker-than-expected operating income guidance.

Wall Street is gearing up for a busy week as several major companies prepare to report earnings, including five of the Magnificent 7 – Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL).
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: IonQ Pushes Into Quantum Security with Florida Network Project as Stock Falls
data.description:
IonQ, Inc. (IONQ) is moving into quantum network tech with a new deal in Florida aimed at building a secure data system.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The company said it signed an agreement with Florida LambdaRail, a statewide fiber network group, to help create a quantum-safe network across the state. The first phase will build a nearly 100-mile link between three research sites from Palm Beach to Miami.

Meanwhile, IONQ shares rose 2.69% on Monday, closing at $43.84. However, the stock dropped by more than 3% in pre-market trading.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: 2 Vanguard ETFs Beating the S&P 500 in 2026 — And Why the Rally Isn't Over
data.description:
There's a clear framework behind stock selection, one that ETFs – particularly actively managed funds – aim to apply in pursuit of consistent outperformance.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Some ETFs are large, low-cost vehicles designed to track broad indices, while others take a more targeted approach and charge higher fees. As those costs rise, however, so do expectations, with investors demanding stronger returns to justify the added expense.

The Vanguard U.S. Momentum Factor ETF (VFMO) and the Vanguard Industrials ETF (VIS) are two such options that have both managed to surpass the rest of the market, with year-to-date total returns of 16% and 13%, respectively – well ahead of the S&P 500.

Though VFMO is a relatively small ETF with $1.56 billion in assets under management, it counts more than 650 holdings in its portfolio. The ETF sources its investments from all corners of the U.S. equity market, providing a healthy mix of various market capitalizations, sectors, and industries.
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Why Joby Aviation Stock (JOBY) Is Up Today – and Why Analysts Assign Street-High $18 Price Target (100% Upside) Ahead of Earnings
data.description:
Electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) leader Joby Aviation (JOBY) is set to report its first-quarter 2026 results on May 5. Ahead of the company's Q1 earnings on May 5, analysts at Needham and H.C. Wainwright have reaffirmed their bullish outlook, maintaining a "Street-high" price target that suggests nearly 100% upside. They cited Joby's growing lead in the air taxi market and its progress toward commercial operations as key drivers.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for JOBY bulls

Interestingly, JOBY stock is seeing upward momentum in Tuesday's pre-market trading following a high-profile demonstration in New York. The company successfully flew its S4 aircraft from JFK Airport to Manhattan, a trip that usually takes over an hour by car, in roughly 10 minutes.

Needham Sees Strong Execution and Industry Leadership

Needham analyst Chris Pierce reiterated a Buy rating on JOBY stock and an $18.00 price target on Joby. After attending the company's NYC kick-off event, Pierce noted that the real-world capabilities shown at JFK "further cement" the future of air taxi travel.

The analyst said these demonstrations reinforce the view that air taxi travel is becoming a matter of "when, not if." He also highlighted Joby's growing lead over peers, citing key milestones such as hybrid VTOL flights and new partnerships, including work with a luxury real estate developer to integrate air taxi access into properties.

H.C. Wainwright Focuses on the Commercial Ramp-Up

H.C. Wainwright analyst Amit Dayal also reaffirmed a Buy rating and an $18.00 price target. Dayal believes the NYC flights place Joby in a dominant position as it prepares for the eIPP program (government-backed flight activities) starting this summer.

According to Dayal, Joby has officially entered its "transition phase" to become a certified commercial operator. He expects investor focus to now shift toward the following:

  • Manufacturing Capacity: How quickly the company can ramp up aircraft production.
  • Vertiport Infrastructure: The development of landing sites in key urban markets.
  • Strategic Backing: Leveraging deep partnerships with Toyota (TM) and Uber (UBER) as the company enters its commercial launch phase.

Is Joby Aviation Stock a Good Buy? 

On TipRanks, JOBY stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with two Buys, four Holds and two Sells assigned in the last three months. The average JOBY stock price target is $13.25, suggesting a potential upside of 46.57% from the current level.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Robinhood (HOOD) Stock Options Traders Are Pricing in a $7.2B Swing after Earnings
data.description:
Robinhood (HOOD) options traders are pricing in a sizable 9.59% move ahead of earnings, which equates to $8.00 per share or about a $7.2 billion swing. The $84 straddle is priced at about $8.15, using the $3.80 call and $4.35 put, while the $85 straddle is $8.22, using the $3.37 call and $4.85 put. As a result, traders are implying a post-earnings range of $75.70 to $91.70, based on current pricing.

data.title: Stock Market News, 4/28/26 – U.S Stock Futures Steady as Wall Street Awaits Iran Peace Updates and Tech Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were steady early Tuesday morning as investors awaited Washington's reaction to peace offers from Iran and earnings results from five of the "Magnificent Seven" tech giants. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.38% and 0.11%, respectively, while the  Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures were up 0.18% at 5:15 a.m. EDT on April 28.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

In a positive move, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed on Monday that Donald Trump and his national security team are reviewing Iran's proposal. The plan offers to reopen the Strait of Hormuz if current conflicts end and U.S. sanctions are lifted

Markets are now closely watching for any signs of a diplomatic breakthrough that could stabilize global energy supply and cool oil prices. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was up by 2.81% to $111.27 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) gained 3.12% to $99.38.

In Monday's regular trading session, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq set new intraday and closing records to kick off a busy week, with the indices up 0.1% and 0.2%, respectively, while the Dow slipped 0.1%.

Wall Street is gearing up for a busy week as several major companies prepare to report earnings. The spotlight will be on tech giants like Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL). Investors will also get updates from companies, including Coca-Cola (KO), General Motors (GM), BP (BP), Spotify (SPOT), Bloom Energy (BE), Robinhood (HOOD), and T-Mobile US (TMUS).

On the economic front, the April consumer confidence report and the S&P Case-Shiller home price index reading will be released on Tuesday.

Elsewhere, European markets edged lower on Tuesday as investors awaited Washington's response to Iran's peace proposals and reacted to earnings from companies including Novartis (NVS), Airbus (AIR), BP (BP), and Barclays (BCS).

Asia-Pacific Markets Mostly Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly lower on Tuesday as investors assessed uncertainty in U.S.-Iran negotiations.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index slipped 0.95%, while Japan's Topix rose 0.99% and the Nikkei 225 fell 1.02%. In China, the Shanghai Composite declined 0.19%, and the Shenzhen Component dropped 1.10%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: XRP Fee Collapse Creates a $1.00 Death Spiral as Network Usage Dries Up with Shocking Speed
data.description:
The price of XRP (XRP-USD) is sitting at a dangerous junction on Tuesday, April 28, 2026. While big-money investors are still buying into funds, the actual network is starting to look like an empty shell. The coin is currently trading near $1.39, but a massive drop in network activity suggests the floor might be about to fall out.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

XRP's Network Fee Decay Creates a Ghost Town Warning

The actual usage of the system is drying up at a shocking speed. The money earned from fees on the network hit a tiny $14,000 this month, which is a total collapse compared to the $440,000 it made last year. Fewer people are using their digital addresses, and the total value sitting in the system has fallen to just $48 million. This makes the project look very small in a market where other systems handle billions of dollars every single day.

Even with the network looking empty, big investors are still pouring cash into the coin. Exchange-traded funds (ETFs) for XRP are having their best month of the year. These funds added over $81 million in assets this month, which is a huge turnaround from the $31 million that investors pulled out last month.

This shows a strange split between people betting on the price and people actually using the technology.

XRP's Dangerous Chart Patterns Create a Target for a $1.00 Floor

The price charts are showing very scary signs for anyone holding the coin right now. Traders see a "mini death cross" forming, which is a pattern that usually points to a major price crash. The coin is trapped in a shape that suggests it will break through its current support level very soon.

If this happens, the next stop is a drop all the way down to $1.00. This downward trend only stops if the price can manage to climb back above $1.60.

At the time of writing, XRP's price is sitting around $1.38.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Is Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ) Falling Today, 4/28/2026?
data.description:
The Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ), which tracks the performance of the Nasdaq 100 Index (NDX), was down 1.16% in Tuesday's pre-market trading, after a Wall Street Journal report highlighted weakness in OpenAI's revenue and new user growth. Reportedly, OpenAI CFO Sarah expressed concerns that the AI startup may not be able to pay computing contracts in the future if its revenue doesn't grow fast enough. This led to declines in the stocks of several AI companies, including Oracle (ORCL), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Broadcom (AVGO), and Nvidia (NVDA) in Tuesday's pre-market trading.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Also, oil prices continued to rise amid tensions in the Middle East.

Meanwhile, the QQQ ETF gained 0.05% on Monday as traders looked past stalled talks between the U.S. and Iran. Overall, the QQQ ETF has risen 2.45% over the past five days and is up 40.5% over the past year.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
